DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. OLEG I. ALBERT, Esq. of Le Clerc & Le Clerc LLP (Tel: 415-445-0900) for plaintiff;
Atty. LEE E. SHELDON, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 415-433-1940) for defendants Hearst Communications Inc. and Sean Kurysh.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SEAN KURYSH AND HEARST COMMUNICATIONS INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

"Defendants' demurrer to plaintiff's complaint" is overruled

Its first sentence applies Labor Code 1102.5 to: "An employer, or any person acting on behalf of an employer" Thus, the statute's plain language provides that Vice President of Human Resources Sean Kurysh may be liable thereunder, and defendants cite no decision of our California courts holding to the contrary.

Further, the complaint's whistleblower retaliation allegations are adequate for the pleading stage. (See, e.g., Cmplt. 1:14-16, 3:14-17, 4:1-26, 6:23-27, 7:24-8:5, 14:11-16:2.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR No. 13859, Email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Tel: 714-471-2597 (302/RBU)
